text: This Key Event Relationship describes how disrupted lipid storage in the liver results in the accumulation of fatty acids.  Disruption of lipid storage and transport can be identified by excess accumulation of fatty acids or other lipids in the liver or altered ratios of expected lipid species which can ultimately lead to liver steatosis (Ipsen et al. 2018).  Disruption of lipid metabolism through dysregulation of transcriptional control and/or decreased or impaired mitochondrial β-oxidation can result in improper lipid storage and an accumulation of fatty acids in liver cells (Ament et al. 2012; Cherkaoui-Malki et al. 2012; Fromenty 2019; Dixon et al. 2021; Xiao et al. 2021).
